name: Roast Leg of Lamb
recipeInstructions:
1. Trim the excess fat from the leg of lamb.
2. Cut slits about 1/2 inch deep all over the lamb and insert slivers of garlic into each slit.
3. Rub the lamb thoroughly with olive oil, ensuring it is evenly coated.
4. Combine salt, pepper, rosemary, and thyme in a small bowl. Rub this mixture generously all over the lamb.
5. Let the lamb sit at room temperature for 20 minutes to allow the seasoning to penetrate.
6. Preheat the oven to 450 degrees Fahrenheit (232 degrees Celsius).
7. Roast the lamb in the preheated oven for 15 minutes at 450 degrees Fahrenheit.
8. Reduce the oven temperature to 350 degrees Fahrenheit (177 degrees Celsius).
9. Continue roasting until the desired level of doneness is reached, which typically takes about an hour for medium rare. Baste with pan juices once or twice during cooking.
10. Remove the lamb from the oven and let it rest at room temperature for 15-20 minutes before carving.
11. Potatoes, carrots, and onions may be roasted in the same pan as the lamb, basting occasionally.
Serving Ideas: Serve with gravy and/or mint jelly.
